patches.find: Rosales-Linares et al. (2019), analyzing Schumann resonance data during geomagnetic storms at a Mexican station, found statistically significant amplitude enhancement across all five G2-G4 events studied (DOI: 10.1016/j.jastp.2018.08.006).
patches.replace: Pazos et al. (2019), analyzing Schumann resonance data at the Coeneo, Michoacán station during geomagnetically disturbed periods, found a statistically significant increase (>1σ) in the averaged amplitude of the first three SR modes across five 14-day periods covering moderate-to-severe geomagnetic storms (Dst < ~−70 nT) (DOI: 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091).
patches.rationale: Fabricated citation: Rosales-Linares does not exist as a SR author. Correct author: Pazos et al. 2019. Correct DOI: 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091. Also corrected finding: paper reports averaged amplitude across 5 periods, not per-event enhancement in all five G2-G4 events. Added station specificity (Coeneo, Michoacán).

patches.find: Either the dome's Kp-detection code has a bug, or the author chose not to score this event. Both undermine the narrative of fully automated, no-cherry-picking verification — the monitor built to test this prediction cannot even detect the relevant storm class.
patches.replace: Either the Kp-detection logic missed the event, the author chose not to score it, or the dashboard caches stale text despite the live-data claim. All three undermine the narrative of fully automated, no-cherry-picking verification — the monitor built to test this prediction cannot even detect the relevant storm class.
patches.rationale: Fix false dichotomy (bug or cherry-pick) to trichotomy. Stale cached dashboard text is a third possibility a dome defender could exploit.

patches.find: Published research (Rosales-Linares et al. 2019) more commonly finds enhancement, not suppression, and the dome's own monitor reports "no G3+ storm observed" for the March event despite NOAA confirmation.
patches.replace: Published research (Pazos et al. 2019, DOI 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091) finds statistically significant SR amplitude increases — not suppression — across storm periods at a Mexican station, and the dome's own monitor reports "no G3+ storm observed" for the March event despite NOAA confirmation.
patches.rationale: Fix fabricated citation in TLDR. Correct author, correct DOI, corrected finding direction.

patches.find: at the equatorial ring (r = 14,105 km), n ≈ 1.83.
patches.rationale: SEC-1.5 still had the stale r=14,105 km equatorial value from before ISS-855. Sections 1.8 and 1.9 both use r≈10,008 km, n=1.44. Harmonizing removes the internal inconsistency that gives dome defenders an easy attack.

patches.find: The dome describes gravity as "unidirectional dielectric acceleration" arising from aetheric pressure between the firmament and the surface.
patches.replace: The dome describes gravity as arising from "dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation" (Domain 4, live-power dashboard) — aetheric medium between the firmament and the disc surface.
patches.rationale: The quoted phrase 'unidirectional dielectric acceleration' does not appear in any dome source. Actual dome phrasing from Domain 4 is 'dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation'. Removing fabricated quoted attribution.

patches.find: six binary tests with the dome's stated rule that failure of any single test falsifies the model.
patches.replace: binary tests with the dome's stated rule that failure of any single test falsifies the model.
patches.rationale: Part 5 Verdict Summary table has SEVEN rows (dome declared six; we added Sydney-Eratosthenes). Dropping the hardcoded count to avoid contradiction.

patches.find: None produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry and differs from standard physics.
patches.replace: None produces a dome-derived prediction that both differs from standard physics and is confirmed by measurement.
patches.rationale: The original sublabel is contradicted by wins.json data: WIN-044 and WIN-057 are tagged derives_from_dome:true. Adding 'confirmed by measurement' as a third condition makes the 0 count accurate — no dome-geometry derivation differs from standard physics AND is confirmed by measurement.

patches.find: The κ coupling constant is post-hoc fitted from one extreme event, not derived from dome geometry. The claimed r = 0.992 correlation is entirely expected: Monte Carlo simulation shows shared atmospheric forcing during a G5 storm produces r > 0.96 (SNR ≥ 5). The dome's two r-values (0.96 and 0.992) for the same dataset are mutually inconsistent
patches.replace: The κ coupling constant is post-hoc fitted from one extreme event, not derived from dome geometry. The claimed r = 0.992 correlation during the October 2003 G5 storm is unremarkable — Monte Carlo shows shared electromagnetic forcing produces r > 0.96 at physically realistic SNR — and the dome itself labels that figure as a temporal correlation of raw L1A residuals, not a slope fit of a coupling law. Neither the r-value nor the 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 ratio is computed by any script in the dome repository.
patches.rationale: ISS-965 / EXP-160 integration: pivot from 'mutually inconsistent r-values' (contestable) to 'labeled temporal correlation + uncomputed' (uncontestable). r=0.96 attribution to current dome pages was found stale by analyst investigation.

patches.find: The coupling constant κ = 1.677 (dome rounds to 1.67) is a post-hoc ratio from one extreme geomagnetic event, not a dome derivation. The claimed r = 0.992 correlation is unremarkable: Monte Carlo simulation shows any two sensors sharing a G5 storm driver produce r > 0.96 at physically realistic SNR. The dome's own pages cite r = 0.96 (PRED-KAPPA-001) and r = 0.992 (Exhibit A) for the same data — two inconsistent values with no computation backing either.
patches.replace: The coupling constant κ = 1.677 (dome rounds to 1.67) is a post-hoc ratio from one extreme geomagnetic event, not a dome derivation. The claimed r = 0.992 correlation is unremarkable: Monte Carlo shows any two sensors sharing a G5 storm driver produce r > 0.96 at physically realistic SNR, and the dome itself labels that figure as a temporal correlation of raw L1A residuals — exactly what standard electromagnetic-artifact physics predicts during storms. No repository code actually computes either the r-value or the 10.9/6.5 ratio; both are static HTML.
patches.rationale: ISS-965 / EXP-160 integration: update tldr_evidence to match new framing.

patches.find: The coupling constant is a post-hoc ratio from one event, not a dome derivation. The predicted and actual correlation values don't match, and the WIN number collides with a completely different claim.
patches.replace: The coupling constant is a post-hoc ratio from one event, not a dome derivation. The correlation it points to is expected from shared electromagnetic forcing during a G5 storm, and the WIN number also collides with a completely different claim on the dome site.
patches.rationale: ISS-965 / EXP-160 integration: update tldr_verdict to remove stale 'predicted and actual values don't match' framing.

patches.find: The prospective claim is internally inconsistent. PRED-KAPPA-001 claims r = 0.96 confirmation of the 1.67 slope, but Exhibit A on the same home page claims r = 0.992 for the same 2003 Halloween storm data. The dome provides no specification of what each r-value measures — they could represent different statistical quantities (slope-fit R² versus temporal cross-correlation). Neither value has a computation in the dome repository — like the 95.2% accuracy figure, they exist only as static HTML text.
patches.replace: The published r-value is labeled, but not computed. The current dome home page (V51.1, audited 2026-04-14) presents the 2003 Halloween-storm figure in Exhibit A as "Correlation (r): 0.992 (Raw L1A residuals)." The parenthetical specifies the statistic: a temporal cross-correlation of level-1 post-residual time series, not a slope-fit R2 of any coupling law. No repository code takes the INTERMAGNET BOU and SG MB/MC series, runs a correlation, and outputs the published number — like the 95.2% accuracy figure (since replaced by a hardcoded 94.5%), the r-value exists only as static HTML text. Readers are asked to accept the statistic without the computation that would allow independent verification.
patches.rationale: ISS-965 / EXP-160: pivot from 'mutually inconsistent r-values' to 'labeled temporal correlation + uncomputed'. Removes contestable inconsistency argument; adds defensible labeled-but-uncomputed argument. Also the current text contains a duplicate 'Note on dome-site numbering' paragraph — fixed in detail_evidence replacement.

patches.find:
